nameMeanings.name: d'Evreux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, Sean.
English and Irish: of Norman origin, a habitational name for someone from Évreux in Eure, France, with the preposition d(e). See also Everest for the name without the preposition.

History: This was the surname of the Earls of Essex from 1572 to 1641. Walter Devereux, 1st Earl of Essex, led an attempted colonization of Ulster, and his son, Robert, 2nd Earl of Essex, briefly held the position of lord lieutenant of Ireland in 1599.

variants.name: Devereaux
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Devereux  .

variants.name: Everest
meanings: English (southeastern): of Norman origin, a variant of Devereux without the fused preposition (Old French de). Compare Evers .

variants.name: Evers
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Devereux without the fused preposition (Old French de).

English: de-aspirated variant of Heaver, with post-medieval excrescent -s, a habitational name from Hever (Kent), named from Old English hēah ‘high’ + (Kentish) Old English ēfre ‘edge or brow of a hill’. Alternatively, the name may derive from some minor place in southeastern England named with Old English ȳfre, (Kentish) Old English ēfre, Middle English evere ‘edge or brow of a hill, tip of a promontory’, with the addition of a prosthetic H-.
North German and Dutch: genitivized patronymic from the personal name Evert , which lost its t in pronunciation and spelling subsequently; compare Everts . Compare also Avers .

variants.name: Deveraux
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Devereux  .

variants.name: Devery
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Kieran, Brendan, Clancy.
Irish (Offaly): of Norman origin, a variant of   Devereux  .

variants.name: Devary
meanings: Probably Irish: variant of Devery, a variant of Devereux in counties Leix and Offaly.

variants.name: Debrow
meanings: Altered form of English or Irish   Devereux  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnès
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: Agnès d'Evreux was born in 0985, in Évreux, Eure, Upper Normandy, France as the daughter of Robert de Normandie and Herleva of Falaise. She had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Gérard de Flaitel. She died in 1027, in Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany, France, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Agnès d'Evreux est née en 0985, à Évreux, Eure, Haute-Normandie, France, fille de Robert de Normandie et Herleva of Falaise. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Gérard de Flaitel. Elle est décédée en 1027, à Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, France, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Agnès d'Evreux wurde 0985 in Évreux, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich, als Tochter von Robert de Normandie und Herleva of Falaise geboren. Sie hatte mit Gérard de Flaitel mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1027 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Agnès d'Evreux è nata il nel 0985 nel Évreux, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia come figlia di Robert de Normandie e Herleva of Falaise. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie con Gérard de Flaitel. Morì nel 1027, nel Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Francia, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnès d'Evreuxは0985年にフランスハウテ-ノーマンディエウアevレウックスでRobert de NormandieとHerleva of Falaiseの娘として生まれた。彼女はGérard de Flaitelとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1027年にフランスブレタグンイレ-エト-ヴィレアインチャートレス-デ-ブレタグンで42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnès d'Evreux(은)는 0985년 프랑스 하테-노먼디 우르 e브레우스에서 Robert de Normandie(와)과 Herleva of Falaise의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Gérard de Flaitel(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1027년 프랑스 브레타그니 이레-트-빌레이인 처트레스-데-브레타그니에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Agnès d'Evreux nasceu em 0985, em Évreux, Eure, Haute-Normandie, França filha de Robert de Normandie e Herleva of Falaise. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas com Gérard de Flaitel. Ela faleceu em 1027, em Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, França, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Agnès d'Evreux родилась в 0985 г. в местности eвреукс, эур, хауте-норманди, Франция. Её родителей звали Robert de Normandie и Herleva of Falaise. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Gérard de Flaitel. Она умерла в 1027 г. в местности чартрес-де-бретагн, илле-ет-вилеин, бретагн, Франция в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Agnès d'Evreux nació en 0985, en Évreux, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia como hija de Robert de Normandie y Herleva of Falaise. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas con Gérard de Flaitel. Murió en 1027, en Chartres-de-Bretagne, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Francia, a la edad de 42 años.
